column1: #   O <POINT cell>~ → sets Output-Range top-left anchor
#   G               → computes and writes the labeled output table
# Seed:  X = A1..A5 = 1..5, Y = B1..B5 = 2..10 step 2  (perfect line y=2x)
# Output anchor: D1.  Labels in column D, scalar values in column E,
# X-coefficient row's value in column E (single X for now).
# X = X-Range → POINT A1..A5 → Enter → back to Regression menu.
# Y = Y-Range → POINT B1..B5 → Enter → back to Regression menu.
# Pointer is at A5 from the X commit; Esc un-anchors so we can
# navigate fresh to B1, anchor with `.`, then extend down to B5.
# O = Output-Range → POINT D1 → Enter.  Pointer position alone
# matters here (only the top-left of the chosen range is used);
# any prior anchor is ignored by the commit handler.
# Output table — the labels in column D are wider than the default
# 9-char column and would render truncated; we assert via the panel-
# line-1 readout (which shows the full source) by parking the cursor
# on each label. Numeric values fit and are checked with ASSERT_CELL.
# Spot-check a couple of the labels via the full-source readout.
